heading: Benefits for persons disabled by treatment or vocational rehabilitation
label: 1151 Benefits for persons disabled by treatment or vocational rehabilitation
notes: References in TextThe date of the enactment of this paragraph, referred to in subsec. (b)(2), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 108–454Dec. 10, 2004

Amendments2004—Subsec. (b). , designated existing provision as par. (1), inserted “(except as otherwise provided in paragraph (2))” after “service-connected, then”, and added par. (2).Pub. L. 108–454, § 304(c)Subsec. (c). , added subsec. (c).Pub. L. 108–454, § 304(a)2000—Subsec. (a)(2). inserted “(A)” after “proximately caused” and added cl. (B).Pub. L. 106–4191996—Subsec. (a). , added subsec. (a) and struck out former first sentence of section which read as follows: “Where any veteran shall have suffered an injury, or an aggravation of an injury, as the result of hospitalization, medical or surgical treatment, or the pursuit of a course of vocational rehabilitation under chapter 31 of this title, awarded under any of the laws administered by the Secretary, or as a result of having submitted to an examination under any such law, and not the result of such veteran’s own willful misconduct, and such injury or aggravation results in additional disability to or the death of such veteran, disability or death compensation under this chapter and dependency and indemnity compensation under chapter 13 of this title shall be awarded in the same manner as if such disability, aggravation, or death were service-connected.”Pub. L. 104–204, § 422(a)(1)Subsec. (b). , designated second sentence of section as subsec. (b), struck out “, aggravation,” after “disability” in two places, and substituted “this subsection equals the total amount” for “this sentence equals the total amount”.Pub. L. 104–204, § 422(a)(2)1991—, renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 102–83, § 5(a)section 351 of this title, substituted “administered by the Secretary” for “administered by the Veterans’ Administration”.Pub. L. 102–83, § 4(a)(1)1984— substituted “title 28” for “title 28, United States Code,” in two places.Pub. L. 98–2231976— struck out “him” before “under any of the laws” and substituted “such veteran’s” for “his” in first sentence.Pub. L. 94–4331969— substituted “, on or after ,” for “hereafter” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 91–24December 1, 19621962— provided that where an individual is awarded a judgment under , enters a settlement or compromise under section 2672 or 2677 of such title by reason of a disability, aggravation, or death treated pursuant to this section as if service-connected, then no benefits shall be paid such individual for any month beginning after such judgment, settlement or compromise becomes final until the aggregate amount of benefits equals the total amount included in such judgment, settlement, or compromise, and struck out provisions which required that no benefits were to be awarded unless application was made therefor within two years after an injury or aggravation was suffered, or a death occurred.Pub. L. 87–825section 1346(b) of title 28

Effective Date of 2004 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–454, title III, § 304(b)118 Stat. 3611Dec. 10, 2004“Subsection (c) of , United States Code, as added by subsection (a), shall apply with respect to eligibility for benefits and services provided by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs on or after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”section 1151 of title 38Dec. 10, 2004

Effective Date of 1996 Amendment, (c), , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–204, title IV, § 422(b)110 Stat. 2927Sept. 26, 1996“(b)(1)The amendments made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall take effect on .October 1, 1996“(2), United States Code (as amended by subsection (a)), shall govern all administrative and judicial determinations of eligibility for benefits under such section that are made with respect to claims filed on or after the effective date set forth in paragraph (1) [], including those based on original applications and applications seeking to reopen, revise, reconsider, or otherwise readjudicate on any basis claims for benefits under such section 1151 or any provision of law that is a predecessor of such section.Section 1151 of title 38Oct. 1, 1996“(c)Nothwithstanding [sic] subsection (b)(1), section 421(d) [set out as a note under ], or any other provision of this Act [see Tables for classification], section 421 [enacting sections 1801 to 1806 of this title, amending , and enacting provisions set out as notes under ] and this section [amending this section] shall not take effect until , unless legislation other than this Act is enacted to provide for an earlier effective date.”section 1801 of this titlesection 5312 of this titlesection 1801 of this titleOctober 1, 1997

children.label: A
children.text: carelessness, negligence, lack of proper skill, error in judgment, or similar instance of fault on the part of the Department in furnishing the hospital care, medical or surgical treatment, or examination; or

children.label: B
children.text: an event not reasonably foreseeable; or

the disability or death was proximately caused (A) by the provision of training and rehabilitation services by the Secretary (including by a service-provider used by the Secretary for such purpose under ) as part of an approved rehabilitation program under chapter 31 of this title, or (B) by participation in a program (known as a “compensated work therapy program”) under .section 3115 of this titlesection 1718 of this title

Where an individual is, on or after , awarded a judgment against the United States in a civil action brought pursuant to or, on or after , enters into a settlement or compromise under section 2672 or 2677 of title 28 by reason of a disability or death treated pursuant to this section as if it were service-connected, then (except as otherwise provided in paragraph (2)) no benefits shall be paid to such individual for any month beginning after the date such judgment, settlement, or compromise on account of such disability or death becomes final until the aggregate amount of benefits which would be paid but for this subsection equals the total amount included in such judgment, settlement, or compromise.December 1, 1962December 1, 1962section 1346(b) of title 28

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of such award shall be reduced by the offset amount; and

children.label: B
children.text: if the offset amount is greater than the amount of such award, the excess amount received pursuant to the judgment, settlement or compromise, shall be offset against benefits otherwise payable under this chapter.
